Cucumber beetles were certainly present, but they didn’t noticeably destroy any plant (other than melon). This may be because we planted nasturtium with cucurbits in Bed #1

Cucumber beetle deterrent: 1) Cucumber beetles often overwinter near to the previous years’ cucurbit crop. So, one way to reduce pest problems the next year is to plant cucurbits as far away from last year’s crop as possible. 2) Apply straw mulch around cucurbits to encourage predator spiders and to keep beetles off of soil where they lay eggs. 3) Intercrop with radish, nasturtium, tansy, buckwheat, cowpea or sweet clover

26 degree temps in forecast, September 8th. We covered 4 tomato plants with brutes and a sheet of plastic. We left the cover on for a few days after the cold weather passed. The tomato plants lived, but the resulting tomatoes were scarred and soft—maybe we “cooked” them under all the plastic?
